<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

```yaml
# =============================================================================
# AGENT WORKING MEMORY — Food Delivery Platform
# =============================================================================
# This file is the agent's live working memory for a single session or task.
# Update every section as work progresses. Never leave stale state.
# Restricted write paths (never modify): .github/workflows/*, docker-compose.yml,
# openapi.yaml, .env.example, agent-context.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
# =============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. SESSION STATE
# Tracks the top-level goal and where the agent currently is within it.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
session_state:
  task_id: null                    # Short unique identifier for this task, e.g. "add-driver-rating-endpoint"
  task_description: null           # Full description of what the agent was asked to do
  status: idle                     # idle | in_progress | blocked | awaiting_review | complete | failed
  phase: null                      # planning | implementation | testing | review | done
  started_at: null                 # ISO 8601 timestamp when task began
  last_updated_at: null            # ISO 8601 timestamp of last memory update
  active_service: null             # Which service is currently being modified:
                                   # api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service |
                                   # driver-service | notification-service | frontend
  active_language: null            # Kotlin | Java | Python | Go | TypeScript
                                   # Must match the active_service's actual language
  active_build_tool: null          # gradle | maven | pip/pytest | go | npm
                                   # api-gateway=gradle, order-service=maven,
                                   # inventory-service=pip, driver-service=go, notification-service/frontend=npm
  scope_summary: null              # One-sentence summary of what files/layers will be touched
  blocking_reason: null            # If status=blocked, why. Otherwise null.


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. DECISIONS MADE
# Every non-trivial design or implementation decision must be recorded here
# with a rationale. This prevents re-litigating settled choices.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
decisions_made: []
# Each entry follows this schema:
# - decision_id: string            # Short slug, e.g. "use-redis-lock-for-stock"
#   timestamp: string              # ISO 8601
#   service: string                # Which service/layer this decision applies to
#   component: string              # Key component affected (from key_components list)
#   summary: string                # One sentence: what was decided
#   rationale: string              # WHY — reference constraints, pitfalls, or domain rules
#   alternatives_considered:       # Other options that were rejected
#     - option: string
#       rejected_because: string
#   affects_openapi: bool          # true if this changes a public API contract in openapi.yaml
#   affects_rabbitmq: bool         # true if this changes exchange names, routing keys, or message shape
#   affects_auth: bool             # true if this touches JWT, X-Customer-Id, or gateway filter logic
#   affects_state_machine: bool    # true if this adds/removes/reorders OrderStatus transitions
#
# Example (uncomment to use):
# - decision_id: "idempotency-in-memory"
#   timestamp: "2024-01-15T10:23:00Z"
#   service: notification-service
#   component: "Notification Service"
#   summary: "Kept idempotency key store as in-memory Set rather than Redis"
#   rationale: >
#     Switching to Redis would add an external dependency to the notification service.
#     The existing in-memory Set (orderId+status composite key) is sufficient for the
#     current single-instance deployment. Known pitfall: resets on restart.
#   alternatives_considered:
#     - option: "Store idempotency keys in Redis"
#       rejected_because: "Adds operational complexity; not required by current task scope"
#   affects_openapi: false
#   affects_rabbitmq: false
#   affects_auth: false
#   affects_state_machine: false


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. FILES MODIFIED
# Tracks every file touched in this session. Includes pre-change context so
# the agent can reason about diffs and revert if needed.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
files_modified: []
# Each entry follows this schema:
# - path: string                   # Repo-relative path, e.g. "order-service/src/main/java/..."
#   service: string                # Which microservice owns this file
#   language: string               # File's language: Kotlin | Java | Python | Go | TypeScript | YAML
#   change_type: string            # added | modified | deleted
#   change_summary: string         # One sentence describing what changed
#   pre_change_state: string       # Brief description of what was there before (or "file did not exist")
#   post_change_state: string      # Brief description of what is there now
#   restricted: bool               # true if this path appears in restricted_write_paths — should always be false
#   test_coverage_added: bool      # true if a corresponding test was added or updated for this change
#   requires_openapi_update: bool  # true if this change alters a public endpoint shape
#   requires_migration: bool       # true if this change alters a DB schema (JPA entity or SQLAlchemy model)
#
# Example (uncomment to use):
# - path: "driver-service/cmd/driver/handlers.go"
#   service: driver-service
#   language: Go
#   change_type: modified
#   change_summary: "Added GET /drivers/:id/rating endpoint handler"
#   pre_change_state: "File contained CRUD handlers and location update handler; no rating logic"
#   post_change_state: "Added GetDriverRating handler; delegates to DriverStore interface"
#   restricted: false
#   test_coverage_added: true
#   requires_openapi_update: true
#   requires_migration: false


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. TESTS RUN
# Records every test execution in this session: command, outcome, and failures.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
tests_run: []
# Each entry follows this schema:
# - run_id: string                 # e.g. "order-service-unit-01"
#   timestamp: string              # ISO 8601
#   service: string                # Which service's tests were run
#   command: string                # Exact command executed, e.g. "mvn -B verify"
#   working_directory: string      # Directory from which the command was run
#   framework: string              # JUnit5 | pytest | go-test | Jest
#   result: string                 # passed | failed | partial | error
#   total_tests: integer           # Total number of test cases executed
#   passed: integer
#   failed: integer
#   skipped: integer
#   coverage_pct: number | null    # Code coverage percentage if reported; null if not available
#   failing_tests: []              # List of failing test names/IDs; empty if result=passed
#   failure_summary: string | null # Brief description of failures; null if result=passed
#   notes: string | null           # Any relevant context, e.g. "Redis not available; integration tests skipped"
#
# Service-to-command reference (do not modify these):
#   order-service:        mvn -B verify        (run from order-service/)
#   inventory-service:    pytest --tb=short -q (run from inventory-service/)
#   driver-service:       go test ./...        (run from driver-service/)
#   notification-service: npx jest             (run from notification-service/)
#   full stack:           docker-compose up --build


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. OPEN QUESTIONS
# Items that require human review, external information, or a decision
# beyond the agent's authority. Block progress and surface these early.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
open_questions: []
# Each entry follows this schema:
# - question_id: string            # Short slug, e.g. "stripe-webhook-secret-rotation"
#   raised_at: string              # ISO 8601 timestamp
#   raised_during_phase: string    # Which phase surfaced this: planning | implementation | testing | review
#   service: string | null         # Affected service, or null if cross-cutting
#   question: string               # The specific question that needs answering
#   context: string                # Why this question is blocking or important
#   urgency: string                # blocking | high | medium | low
#   resolution: string | null      # Filled in when answered; null while open
#   resolved_at: string | null     # ISO 8601 when resolved; null while open
#
# Examples of questions this project commonly raises (uncomment as needed):
#
# - question_id: "openapi-contract-sync"
#   raised_at: null
#   raised_during_phase: planning
#   service: null
#   question: >
#     The proposed endpoint changes alter the public API contract. Has openapi.yaml
#     been reviewed and approved for update before implementation begins?
#   context: >
#     openapi.yaml is a restricted write path. Changing it requires coordinating
#     all downstream consumers. Agent cannot modify it unilaterally.
#   urgency: blocking
#   resolution: null
#   resolved_at: null
#
# - question_id: "rabbitmq-routing-key-change"
#   raised_at: null
#   raised_during_phase: planning
#   service: notification-service
#   question: >
#     Does the proposed change alter the RabbitMQ exchange name 'order.events'
#     or routing key pattern? If so, notification-service consumer must be
#     updated atomically with order-service publisher.
#   context: >
#     order-service publishes to 'order.events'; notification-service binds to
#     the same exchange. Mismatched names silently drop messages.
#   urgency: blocking
#   resolution: null
#   resolved_at: null


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. DOMAIN STATE
# Language- and framework-specific runtime state worth tracking across steps.
# Organized by service. Update only the sections relevant to the active task.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
domain_state:

  # --- Order Service (Java / Spring Boot / Maven) ----------------------------
  order_service:
    order_status_machine:
      # Current known valid transitions (from OrderStatus.java).
      # Update here if isValidTransition logic is modified — affects_state_machine=true.
      transitions:
        PENDING:          [ACCEPTED, CANCELLED]
        ACCEPTED:         [PREPARING, CANCELLED]
        PREPARING:        [READY_FOR_PICKUP]
        READY_FOR_PICKUP: [IN_TRANSIT]
        IN_TRANSIT:       [DELIVERED]
        DELIVERED:        []           # terminal
        CANCELLED:        [REFUNDED]
        REFUNDED:         []           # terminal
      statuses_added_this_session: []  # Any new OrderStatus enum values added
      statuses_removed_this_session: []
    stripe_integration:
      payment_gateway_interface_intact: true  # Must remain true — removing abstraction is forbidden
      new_payment_operations_added: []        # e.g. ["capturePayment"]
    rabbitmq:
      exchange_name: "order.events"    # Do not change without updating notification-service
      routing_keys_in_use: []          # Populate if routing keys are added/changed
    db_migrations:
      pending_schema_changes: []       # e.g. ["added column: orders.driver_id"]
      migration_files_added: []

  # --- Inventory Service (Python / FastAPI / asyncpg / Redis) ----------------
  inventory_service:
    async_session_compliance: true     # All DB ops must be awaited with AsyncSession
    redis_locks:
      ttl_seconds: 10                  # Current lock TTL — race condition risk if ops exceed this
      operations_using_locks: []       # Track which endpoints/functions acquire locks
    models_changed: []                 # SQLAlchemy model changes (Restaurant, MenuItem)
                                       # Each change here likely
